title: Indulgent Tunisian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Tunisian dish featuring lamb, balsamic vinegar, and a mirepoix base, all wrapped in a corn husk for a unique and indulgent experience. This low-carb friendly recipe is perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the balsamic vinegar, olive oil, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb pieces to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil over medium heat and cook the onion, carrot, and celery until the vegetables are tender.
Add the garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the cooked vegetable mixture to the bowl with the lamb and toss to combine.
Stir in the chopped dried apricots.
To assemble the tagine, lay a corn husk flat on a work surface and spoon about 1/2 cup of the lamb and vegetable mixture onto the center of the husk.
Fold the sides of the husk over the filling and roll the husk into a tight cylinder, repeating with the remaining ingredients.
Place the tagine rolls in a large baking dish and cover with foil.
Bake the tagine for 2 1/2 hours, until the lamb is tender and the sauce is rich and syrupy.
